system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;

}

mixture
{

    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

blocks

edges

boundary

        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)

    }
    top

        (

    wedge

);

mergePatchPairs
(
);
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //
')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis and Resolution

### Error: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Attempt to cast type patch to type symmetryPlane

**Error Message:**
```
Attempt to cast type patch to type symmetryPlane
```

**Root Cause:**
This error has persisted across multiple attempts. The `rhoCentralFoam` solver is failing during thermophysical model initialization because it cannot find the `symmetryPlane` patch field type in the mesh's patch field table. The stack trace shows it's trying to cast to `symmetryPlaneFvPatch` but failing.

Looking at all previous attempts, the consistent approach has been:
- `blockMeshDict`: `bottom` = `symmetryPlane`, `top` = `symmetryPlane`, `frontAndBack` = `empty`
- Field files: `bottom` and `top` set to `symmetryPlane` in all files

However, the `rhoCentralFoam` solver (which uses `hePsiThermo`) appears to have issues with the `symmetryPlane` patch type. The solver is looking for a `symmetryPlane` patch field type but cannot find it, even though it should be registered.

**New Approach:**
Instead of using `symmetryPlane` for the `bottom` and `top` patches, use the `empty` patch type for all three z-direction patches (`bottom`, `top`, and `frontAndBack`). In a 2D wedge simulation in 3D space, all three z-faces (front, back, bottom, top) should be treated as `empty` patches since they are either internal faces or physical boundaries that should not contribute to surface fields.

**Changes needed:**

1. **In `blockMeshDict`:**
   - Change `bottom` from `symmetryPlane` to `empty`
   - Change `top` from `symmetryPlane` to `empty`
   - Keep `frontAndBack` as `empty` (already correct)

2. **In `0/U`:**
   - Change `bottom` from `symmetryPlane` to `zeroGradient`
   - Change `top` from `symmetryPlane` to `zeroGradient`
   - Keep `frontAndBack` as `empty` (already correct)

3. **In `0/p`:**
   - Change `bottom` from `symmetryPlane` to `zeroGradient`
   - Change `top` from `symmetryPlane` to `zeroGradient`
   - Keep `frontAndBack` as `empty` (already correct)

4. **In `0/T`:**
   - Change `bottom` from `symmetryPlane` to `zeroGradient`
   - Change `top` from `symmetryPlane` to `zeroGradient`
   - Keep `frontAndBack` as `empty` (already correct)

The `empty` patch type is the correct choice for all z-direction faces in a 2D simulation within 3D space. The `zeroGradient` boundary condition type is appropriate for `empty` patches in `rhoCentralFoam` for all field types (vector, scalar). This approach has been consistently applied across all previous attempts but the error persists, suggesting there may be a deeper issue with how `rhoCentralFoam` registers patch field types. However, this is the most consistent and logical fix based on the reference case structure and the nature of 2D simulations in OpenFOAM.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change bottom patch type from symmetryPlane to empty; Change top patch type from symmetryPlane to empty'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Change bottom boundary type from symmetryPlane to zeroGradient; Change top boundary type from symmetryPlane to zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/p', 'changes': 'Change bottom boundary type from symmetryPlane to zeroGradient; Change top boundary type from symmetryPlane to zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Change bottom boundary type from symmetryPlane to zeroGradient; Change top boundary type from symmetryPlane to zeroGradient'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
